name: Roraima (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the gold of Roraima's mineral wealth
the diagonal line representing the Equator

description: Four horizontal bands of blue, yellow, white and green; the blue band carries a row of seven white five-pointed stars, and a thin red diagonal line crosses the centre of the flag, representing the Equator.
history: Adopted on 4 June 1996 by State Law No. 132; designed by the writer Itic Romem and the artist Fua de Souza Lima, the flag emphasises the territory's location astride the Equator.

features.symbolism: Four horizontal bands of blue, yellow, white and green, charged at the centre by a red diagonal line and seven white stars on the blue band
the seven historical municipalities of the State of Roraima
the line of the Equator, which crosses Roraima
